message:
test(raptorq): add structured unit test log emission for decoder verification scenarios

Introduce test-side helpers for emitting structured JSON log entries
from RaptorQ decoder unit tests, following the rq-track-c-decoder-unit-v1
schema. This enables machine-readable test result tracking and
deterministic scenario replay.

New helpers:

- to_unit_decode_stats(): converts DecodeStats into the UnitDecodeStats
  schema struct, capturing k, loss percentage, peeled/inactivated counts,
  Gauss operation counts, pivot selections, peel queue metrics, dense
  core dimensions, and peeling fallback reasons
- emit_decoder_unit_log(): creates a UnitLogEntry with scenario ID, seed,
  parameter set, outcome, repro command (for rch remote execution), and
  optional decode stats; validates the JSON against the unit log schema
  and asserts zero violations before returning the serialized string

Updated tests:

- decode_insufficient_symbols_fails: now emits a structured failure log
  entry (RQ-C-LOG-FAIL-INSUFFICIENT-001) with artifact path pointer and
  validates the JSON contains the expected scenario ID and artifact path
- decode_deterministic: now emits a success log entry (RQ-C-LOG-SUCCESS-DET-001)
  with full decode stats from the first run, validating outcome marker
  and repro command preservation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
